message:
chore(beads): add graph-ranking phase-1 implementation bead (bd-8ks0.3)

Register new in-progress bead for the non-stub query-biased graph
ranking signal integration in the fusion pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -545,6 +545,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-8ks0","title":"Integrate franken_networkx for graph-aware ranking signal and result diversification","description":"When franken_networkx matures, add a structural/graph-based ranking signal as a third input to the RRF fusion pipeline alongside lexical (BM25) and semantic (cosine). Document collections often have implicit relationship structure — citations, cross-references, thread links, file-system adjacency, import graphs — and exploiting this structure improves search quality for navigational and exploratory queries.

PREREQUISITE — DOCUMENT GRAPH CONSTRUCTION:
Before graph algorithms can run, frankensearch needs a document graph. The graph is:
- Nodes: documents in the index (identified by doc_id)
- Edges: relationships between documents, with types and weights
- Edge sources (consumers provide these, frankensearch doesn't infer them):
  a. Explicit: consumer calls graph_builder.add_edge(doc_a, doc_b, EdgeType::Reference, weight=1.0)
  b. Batch: consumer provides adjacency list at index time
  c. File-system: fsfs (bd-2hz) can derive edges from directory co-location and import statements

New type in frankensearch-core:
  pub struct DocumentGraph {
      adjacency: HashMap<DocId, Vec<(DocId, EdgeType, f32)>>,  // neighbor, type, weight
      node_count: usize,
  }
  pub enum EdgeType { Reference, CoLocation, Import, ThreadReply, Similar, Custom(String) }

The DocumentGraph is optional — frankensearch works without it (current behavior). When provided, the graph ranking signal activates automatically.

THREE GRAPH RANKING APPLICATIONS (phased implementation):

PHASE 1 — Query-Biased PageRank (highest impact, implement first):
- Standard PageRank computes global importance. Query-biased (Personalized) PageRank seeds the random walk from query-matched documents, computing importance RELATIVE to the query.
- Algorithm: PPR with restart probability α=0.15, seeded at documents matching the query
- Output: per-document PPR score in [0, 1], feeds into RRF as a third ranking
- Why it helps: boosts documents that are structurally central NEAR the query topic, not just globally important
- Computational cost: O(|E| * iterations), typically 10-20 iterations, <10ms for 10K-node graphs
- franken_networkx dependency: PageRank with personalization vector

PHASE 2 — Result Diversification via Graph Clustering:
- After RRF fusion produces a ranked list, check if top-K results are concentrated in one graph cluster
- Use spectral clustering or community detection (Louvain/Leiden) to identify clusters
- If >60% of top-10 results are from the same cluster, replace some with representatives from other clusters
- This is a POST-FUSION re-ranking step, not a fusion input
- franken_networkx dependency: community detection (Louvain), spectral clustering

PHASE 3 — Document Expansion (query-time neighbor boosting):
- When a query strongly matches document A (high semantic + lexical score), also boost A's graph neighbors
- Boost factor: neighbor_score = original_score * edge_weight * expansion_factor (default 0.3)
- Limit: expand only from top-3 initial matches, only 1-hop neighbors
- This helps for sparse queries where the best result has strong neighbors the user would also want
- franken_networkx dependency: neighborhood queries, edge weight lookup

RRF INTEGRATION MODEL:
- Phase 1 (PPR scores) integrates as a THIRD input to RRF fusion alongside lexical and semantic:
  rrf_score = 1/(K+rank_lexical) + 1/(K+rank_semantic) + weight_graph * 1/(K+rank_graph)
- weight_graph is configurable in TwoTierConfig (default 0.5 — lower than lexical/semantic because graph signal is optional and may not be available)
- When no DocumentGraph is provided, weight_graph = 0 and RRF reduces to current 2-input formula
- Phase 2 runs AFTER RRF (post-fusion diversification)
- Phase 3 runs BEFORE RRF (pre-fusion score boosting)

FEATURE FLAG:
`graph = ['dep:franken_networkx']` in frankensearch-fusion/Cargo.toml
- Off by default
- The `full` meta-feature should include it when franken_networkx is mature

CRATE PLACEMENT:
- DocumentGraph, EdgeType: frankensearch-core/src/graph.rs (zero-dep types)
- GraphRanker (PPR): frankensearch-fusion/src/graph_rank.rs
- Diversifier (clustering): frankensearch-fusion/src/diversify.rs
- DocumentExpander: frankensearch-fusion/src/expand.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Phase 1 (PPR):
1. PPR scores sum to ~1.0 (valid probability distribution)
2. PPR with no seed documents returns uniform scores (degenerates to global PageRank)
3. PPR with seed documents returns higher scores for seed neighbors than distant nodes
4. RRF with 3 inputs (lexical + semantic + graph) produces valid rankings
5. RRF with graph weight=0 produces identical results to current 2-input RRF
6. PPR computation completes in <10ms for 10K-node graph
7. No DocumentGraph provided → graph signal silently disabled, no errors

Phase 2 (Diversification):
8. Top-10 results after diversification span at least 2 clusters (when graph has 3+ clusters)
9. Diversification does not remove highly relevant results (nDCG@10 drop < 5% vs undiversified)
10. Diversification is a no-op when results already span multiple clusters

Phase 3 (Expansion):
11. Expanded results include graph neighbors of top-3 matches
12. Expansion boost factor is correctly applied (neighbor_score = original * weight * factor)
13. Expansion is bounded (only 1-hop, only from top-3, max 10 expanded results)
14. Expansion with no graph → no-op","notes":"Deferred by backlog harmonization pass. Re-entry criteria: (1) Sprint-1 keystone program bd-1zxn closed, (2) release gate bd-ehuk closed, (3) proof-lane contract bd-bobf has at least one completed exemplar, and (4) concrete EV score >= 2.0 with measured hotspot evidence.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:26:04.320039565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:26:13.448831913Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["experimental","future","parking-lot","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8ks0","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:47:50.441077745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-8ks0","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:53:14.117293003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":463,"issue_id":"bd-8ks0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RATIONALE BACKFILL: Added concrete acceptance criteria so future graph-aware ranking integration is measurable, explainable, and reproducible.","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:29:15Z"},{"id":709,"issue_id":"bd-8ks0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN CLARIFICATIONS:

1. DIVERSIFICATION ALGORITHM (Phase 2): When >60% of top-10 results are from the same cluster, the replacement algorithm is: (a) Compute cluster assignments for all top-K candidates (not just top-10), (b) Group results by cluster, (c) Greedily select: pick the highest-scoring result from the underrepresented cluster, swap it for the lowest-scoring same-cluster result in top-10, (d) Repeat until top-10 spans at least 2 clusters or no more candidates exist, (e) Never remove the top-1 result regardless of cluster distribution. This is essentially MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance) with graph distance as the diversity metric.

2. MAGIC NUMBERS AND TUNABILITY: weight_graph (0.5), alpha (0.15 PPR restart probability), expansion_factor (0.3), diversification_threshold (0.6) are ALL configurable in GraphRankConfig. They should be added to the CMA-ES parameter space in bd-2hk9 when graph ranking is enabled.

3. GRAPH PERSISTENCE: DocumentGraph is not persisted by frankensearch — the consumer provides it at index time. For fsfs (bd-2hz): graph is rebuilt on each index refresh from file system adjacency.

4. SCALABILITY: HashMap adjacency is fine for <100K documents (frankensearch's target range). For larger graphs, use CSR (Compressed Sparse Row) representation from franken_networkx. franken_networkx should provide the graph data structure — this bead should NOT reimplement graph storage.

5. SCORE INFLATION IN PHASE 3 (EXPANSION): Expanded neighbor scores are capped: neighbor_score = min(original_score * weight * factor, original_top1_score * 0.9). This ensures expanded results never outrank the original top result. Expanded results are deduplicated: if a neighbor is already in results, keep max(existing_score, expanded_score).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:53:42Z"},{"id":730,"issue_id":"bd-8ks0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"EVIDENCE CLAUSE ADDENDUM: graph-signal work requires unit tests for graph-score correctness, integration tests for fusion behavior, and e2e relevance/diversification scenarios with structured logging, telemetry metrics, and artifact-backed replay traces.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:26:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8ks0.1","title":"Phase 1 scaffold: add optional DocumentGraph and EdgeType core types","description":"Implement the minimal core graph data model for future graph-aware ranking: DocumentGraph adjacency map and EdgeType taxonomy in frankensearch-core, with deterministic helper APIs and unit tests. No fusion integration yet.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureCitadel","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:01:42.209963958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:15:51.221066300Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T02:15:51.221026586Z","close_reason":"Phase 1 DocumentGraph/EdgeType scaffold delivered and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","experimental","graph"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8ks0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-8ks0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:01:42.209963958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1612,"issue_id":"bd-8ks0.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented core graph scaffold in frankensearch-core with optional types and deterministic adjacency helpers: GraphDocId, EdgeType, GraphEdge, and DocumentGraph (plus unit tests and serde roundtrip coverage).

Validation evidence (all heavy cargo via rch):
- rch exec -- cargo fmt -p frankensearch-core -- --check  ✅
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankensearch-core --all-targets  ✅
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankensearch-core --all-targets -- -D warnings  ✅
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankensearch-core graph::tests -- --nocapture  ✅ (5 passed)

Workspace baseline check after this lane:
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets  ✅
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  ❌
  current failures are in frankensearch-index (search.rs + warmup.rs + lib.rs), now tracked in active lane bd-2yu.5.29 by TurquoiseLantern.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:15:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8ks0.2","title":"Phase 1 plumbing: add optional graph ranking channel in fusion (stubbed)","description":"Implement a first vertical slice for bd-8ks0 in frankensearch-fusion: add graph ranking config knobs and optional graph-score channel wiring in fusion/search orchestration behind a safe default-off path. This slice focuses on config/plumbing/test scaffolding (no heavy franken_networkx algorithm dependency yet).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureCitadel","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:24:39.180659060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:40:33.899515590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T02:40:33.899497105Z","close_reason":"Phase-1 graph config/plumbing stub delivered and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["experimental","fusion","graph"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8ks0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-8ks0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:24:39.180659060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1616,"issue_id":"bd-8ks0.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Delivered a minimal phase-1 graph plumbing slice (default-off, no algorithm dependency):

Code changes:
- `crates/frankensearch-core/src/config.rs`
  - added `graph_ranking_enabled: bool` (default `false`)
  - added `graph_ranking_weight: f64` (default `0.5`)
  - added env overrides:
    - `FRANKENSEARCH_GRAPH_RANKING_ENABLED`
    - `FRANKENSEARCH_GRAPH_RANKING_WEIGHT`
  - updated config tests/default/debug/serde assertions for new knobs
- `crates/frankensearch-fusion/Cargo.toml`
  - added feature flag: `graph = []`
- `crates/frankensearch-fusion/src/lib.rs`
  - feature-gated module export: `graph_rank`
  - feature-gated re-export: `GraphRanker`
- `crates/frankensearch-fusion/src/graph_rank.rs` (new)
  - added no-op `GraphRanker` stub for phase-1 wiring
- `crates/frankensearch-fusion/src/searcher.rs`
  - wired optional phase-1 graph candidate hook guarded by config + feature
  - added test `graph_ranking_enabled_stub_is_noop`

Validation evidence:
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankensearch-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankensearch-core default_config_values -- --nocapture` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankensearch-core config_serialization_roundtrip -- --nocapture` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankensearch-fusion graph_ranking_enabled_stub_is_noop -- --nocapture` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankensearch-fusion --features graph --all-targets` ✅

Current shared blocker (outside this bead scope):
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ❌ due unrelated current `frankensearch-index` lint/unsafe failures in files not reserved by this lane (`src/lib.rs`, `src/two_tier.rs`).
","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:40:30Z"}]}
